text.title: Spec-Kit Three-Step Pipeline (specify → plan → tasks) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the task-spec-kit-three-step methodology plus the `skip-this-methodology` safety rule. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The pipeline MUST run `specify` → `plan` → `tasks` in this exact order. `plan.md` MUST NOT exist before `spec.md`; `tasks.md` MUST NOT exist before `plan.md`. A missing predecessor is a hard failure.
rule.rationale: Out-of-order artifacts let the agent ship code without grounding; the order is what makes the spec the durable source of truth.

rule.statement: Every `[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]` marker in `spec.md` MUST be resolved (replaced with a concrete value or dropped with rationale) before `plan` runs. Unresolved markers block the pipeline.
rule.rationale: Unresolved clarifications are how LLM-generated specs leak hallucinated requirements into the plan.

rule.statement: `plan.md` MUST contain a `Constitution Gate` section that cites the standards consulted (filename + section anchor) and explicitly states either compliance or an approved exception.
rule.rationale: Without cited evidence, `plan.md` is opinion; with citations it becomes auditable.

rule.statement: Only `tasks.md` MAY be the coding agent's prompt. The agent MUST NOT consume `spec.md` or `plan.md` as direct instructions — they are reference context only.
rule.rationale: Mixing layers (spec + plan + tasks) into one prompt collapses the abstraction; the agent loses the `what vs how vs work item` boundary.

rule.statement: A task tagged `[P]` MUST be safe to execute in parallel with every other `[P]` task in the same group: no shared file edits, no shared schema migrations. Mis-tagging `[P]` is a hard failure.
rule.rationale: Parallel tagging that is not actually parallel breaks fleet orchestration with silent merge conflicts.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the preconditions in `## Applies If` are not all true, or any item in `## Skip If` holds. The decision tree routes to this rule on those branches.
rule.rationale: A methodology that runs on the wrong input wastes tokens and produces misleading artifacts. The explicit skip rule is the safety valve.
